root_text.an6.46:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahācundo cetīsu viharati sayaṁjātiyaṁ.
root_text.an6.46:1.3: Tatra kho āyasmā mahācundo bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an6.46:1.5: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahācundassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an6.46:2.2: ‘ime pana jhāyinomhā, jhāyinomhāti jhāyanti pajjhāyanti nijjhāyanti avajjhāyanti.
root_text.an6.46:2.3: Kimime jhāyanti, kintime jhāyanti, kathaṁ ime jhāyantī’ti?
root_text.an6.46:2.4: Tattha dhammayogā ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, jhāyī ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, na ca bahujanahitāya paṭipannā honti bahujanasukhāya bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an6.46:3.1: Idha panāvuso, jhāyī bhikkhū dhammayoge bhikkhū apasādenti:
root_text.an6.46:3.2: ‘ime pana dhammayogamhā, dhammayogamhāti uddhatā unnaḷā capalā mukharā vikiṇṇavācā muṭṭhassatī asampajānā asamāhitā vibbhantacittā pākatindriyā.
root_text.an6.46:3.3: Kimime dhammayogā, kintime dhammayogā, kathaṁ ime dhammayogā’ti?
root_text.an6.46:3.4: Tattha jhāyī ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, dhammayogā ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, na ca bahujanahitāya paṭipannā honti bahujanasukhāya bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an6.46:4.1: Idha panāvuso, dhammayogā bhikkhū dhammayogānaññeva bhikkhūnaṁ vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanti, no jhāyīnaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanti.
root_text.an6.46:4.2: Tattha dhammayogā ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, jhāyī ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, na ca bahujanahitāya paṭipannā honti bahujanasukhāya bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an6.46:5.1: Idha panāvuso, jhāyī bhikkhū jhāyīnaññeva bhikkhūnaṁ vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanti, no dhammayogānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanti.
root_text.an6.46:5.2: Tattha jhāyī ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, dhammayogā ca bhikkhū nappasīdanti, na ca bahujanahitāya paṭipannā honti bahujanasukhāya bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an6.46:6.5: Acchariyā hete, āvuso, puggalā dullabhā lokasmiṁ, ye amataṁ dhātuṁ kāyena phusitvā viharanti.
root_text.an6.46:7.5: Acchariyā hete, āvuso, puggalā dullabhā lokasmiṁ ye gambhīraṁ atthapadaṁ paññāya ativijjha passantī”ti.

translation_text.an6.46:1.2: At one time Venerable Mahācunda was staying in the land of the Cetis at Sahajāti.
translation_text.an6.46:2.1: “Take a case where mendicants who practice discernment of principles rebuke mendicants who practice absorption:
translation_text.an6.46:2.2: ‘They say, “We practice absorption meditation! We practice absorption meditation!” And they meditate and concentrate and contemplate and ruminate.
translation_text.an6.46:2.3: Why do they practice absorption meditation? In what way do they practice absorption meditation? How do they practice absorption meditation?’
translation_text.an6.46:2.4: In this case the mendicants who practice discernment of principles are not inspired, and the mendicants who practice absorption are not inspired. And they’re not acting for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.an6.46:3.1: Now, take a case where mendicants who practice absorption meditation rebuke mendicants who practice discernment of principles:
translation_text.an6.46:3.2: ‘They say, “We practice discernment of principles! We practice discernment of principles!” But they’re restless, insolent, fickle, scurrilous, loose-tongued, unmindful, lacking situational awareness and immersion, with straying minds and undisciplined faculties.
translation_text.an6.46:3.3: Why do they practice discernment of principles? In what way do they practice discernment of principles? How do they practice discernment of principles?’
translation_text.an6.46:3.4: In this case the mendicants who practice absorption are not inspired, and the mendicants who practice discernment of principles are not inspired. And they’re not acting for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.an6.46:4.1: Now, take a case where mendicants who practice discernment of principles praise only others like them, not mendicants who practice absorption.
translation_text.an6.46:4.2: In this case the mendicants who practice discernment of principles are not inspired, and the mendicants who practice absorption are not inspired. And they’re not acting for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.an6.46:5.1: And take a case where mendicants who practice absorption praise only others like them, not mendicants who practice discernment of principles.
translation_text.an6.46:5.2: In this case the mendicants who practice absorption are not inspired, and the mendicants who practice discernment of principles are not inspired. And they’re not acting for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.an6.46:6.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an6.46:6.2: ‘As mendicants who practice discernment of principles, we will praise mendicants who practice absorption.’
translation_text.an6.46:6.5: Because it’s rare to find such incredible individuals in the world who have direct meditative experience of the element free of death.
translation_text.an6.46:7.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an6.46:7.2: ‘As mendicants who practice absorption, we will praise mendicants who practice discernment of principles.’
translation_text.an6.46:7.5: Because it’s incredibly rare to find individuals in the world who see the meaning of a deep saying with penetrating wisdom.”

variant_text.an6.46:2.2: nijjhāyanti avajjhāyanti → etthantare pāṭho si, sya-all, pts1ed potthakesu natthi; nijjhāyanti apajjhāyanti (9M:1740)
variant_text.an6.46:2.3: Kimime → kintime (bj); kiñhime (sya-all); kiṁ hime (km, pts1ed)

reference_text.an6.46:1.1: csp1ed16.312, csp2ed16.312, ms16A6_351, msdiv46, pts-vp-pli3.355, vri38.69
